chunks.text: This page lists 2009 technical reports from EECS at UC Berkeley. The reports cover diverse topics including cognitive radios, printed electronics, nanoscale SRAM design, silicon photonics systems, rural wireless networks, actor model code generation, interference exploitation, browser extension security, wireless communication systems, MapReduce optimization, MOSFET replacements, machine learning for system optimization, computer vision reconstruction, DRAM with photonics, stencil code auto-tuning, combinatorial optimization, gas sensor transistors, Datalog in time and space, de novo assembly, biosensor technology, delay-tolerant storage, sparse signal recovery, collective communication tuning, robust system design, wireless sensor network management, nanoparticle manipulation, chaotic circuits, CMOS processes, interactive curve design, beamforming analysis, cloud workload modeling, digit classification, cluster computing substrate, binary program testing, memristor circuits, community healthcare systems, body sensor networks, hybrid routing protocols, spline knots, finite state machines, multipath routing, deadline instructions, distributed event execution, capacitorless DRAM, internet-enabled choice, information flow tracking, uncertain data querying, synchronous data flow composition, communication costs, innovation below APIs, power optimization, Firefox extension APIs, green building algorithms, network incentives, MapReduce online, cyber-physical system models, 60-GHz CMOS circuits, binary code extraction, path slicing, virtual local stores, game-theoretic software analysis, contour detection, fault resilience in multi-core, weigh-in-motion systems, distributed CSMA algorithms, hierarchical planning, brain-machine interfaces, declarative information extraction, functional dependency discovery, silicon photonic devices, real-time system design, coarse-to-fine NLP, circuit symmetries, probabilistic event triggering, data-centric programming, broadband LNA designs, context in intrusion detection, energy-efficient MapReduce, multicore debugging, multi-band receiver designs, quantified domains, internet routing, structured tracking, console log mining, trade space exploration, learning to hash, nanowire growth, LDPC decoder design, object interface enforcement, multiple kernel learning, SMT solver engineering, SVM approximation, quantitative analysis workshop, rural wireless network architecture, declarative networked systems, digital animation curriculum, mobile phone games, photolithography monitoring, human action analysis, Giotto code generation, collaborative filtering algorithms, new communications API, NEM relay memory, heterogeneous system design, sigma-delta converters, quantum circuit fault tolerance, computer science illustration, switched-capacitor converter design, model transformation, real-time code generation, wireless sensor metrics, pentacene thin films, automotive system synthesis, wireless camera networks, sparse coding models, LiDAR data merging, RF ranging, Ethernet encodings, occlusion handling, fast surface reconstruction, nanoscale device interfaces, program transformations, packet classification, block codes with feedback, relational interfaces, on-chip networks, malware emulation, protocol reverse engineering, Escher tile tools, MapReduce job scheduling, filter spreading applications, motor control learning, computational complexity measurement, RF receiver optimization, micro-solar power theory, secure aggregation statistics, channel capacity measurement, sequential logic invariants, spectral clustering, concurrent data structure annotations, CSMA convergence, SPMD program equivalence, string theory satisfiability, distributed file caching, distributed inference algorithms, automatic synthesis for distributed systems, throughput-optimal CSMA, security-sensitive operation modeling, indoor localization, binary program symbolic execution, dynamic scene analysis, real-time embedded systems, computing needs time, Cholesky decomposition, cloud computing overview, long-distance wireless performance, web authentication factors, millimeter-wave amplifiers, grammar construction models, energy-efficient transmitters, queueing network models, Chua's circuit implementation, multiprocessor allocation, flicker noise modeling, co-training with noise, hierarchical object categorization, side channel attack elimination, width inference algorithms, grammar learning bootstrapping, DNA detection transistors, adaptive bandit algorithms, multi-socket photonic systems, CSMA/CA throughput control, disciplined message passing, chaotic memristor circuits, LiDAR surface reconstruction, camera network topology recovery, white space analysis, hidden terminal collision estimation, and array performance with errors.
